facts.excerpt: The Stargate Project intends to invest $500 billion over the next four years building new AI infrastructure for OpenAI in the United States, with $100 billion being deployed immediately.
facts.notes: Stargate JV: SoftBank, OpenAI, Oracle, MGX; 10 GW U.S. buildout announced 2025-01-21. OpenAI is a JV partner, not sole owner. openai.com returned 403 to direct fetch; text confirmed via WebSearch result quoting the OpenAI page.

verification.verifier_note: openai.com/announcing-the-stargate-project (403 to WebFetch; confirmed via openai.com page content in search): 'intends to invest $500 billion over the next four years building new AI infrastructure.' Announced/intended, not deployed. Matches value $500B, status announced, Jan 2025.

facts.excerpt: up to $11.9 billion
facts.notes: Initial CoreWeave-OpenAI infrastructure agreement, up to $11.9B; OpenAI to receive $350M of CoreWeave stock. Later expanded by up to $4B (May 2025) and a further $6.5B (per CoreWeave investor releases), ~$22.4B cumulative. Rented compute = cloud_partnership.

verification.verifier_note: coreweave.com: 'The contract value for this strategic deal is up to $11.9 billion.' Matches $11.9B contracted.

facts.excerpt: Lancium's Abilene campus features a 1.2GW interconnect, which has been fully approved by ERCOT
facts.notes: ERCOT interconnection rights (1.2 GW) secured by Lancium (AEP interconnection study, grid rights obtained 2021); Crusoe operator-tenant. Domain lancium.com (not in scoreable-domain list). Site leased to OpenAI workloads via Oracle.

verification.verifier_note: lancium.com/locations states 'Our Abilene campus features a 1.2GW interconnect, which has been fully approved by ERCOT.' Supports 1200 MW interconnection, approved/contracted.

facts.excerpt: Crusoe Adds 4.5 GW Natural Gas to Fuel AI, Expands Abilene Data Center to 1.2 GW
facts.notes: Flagship Stargate site. Operated by Crusoe Energy on land leased from Lancium; developed with Oracle; OpenAI is workload tenant via Oracle/OCI. NOT OpenAI-owned, hence cloud_partnership. 8 buildings, ~4M sq ft. Epoch reports OpenAI later capped this site at 1.2 GW (dropped a 2.1 GW expansion).

verification.verifier_note: datacenterfrontier article states total power capacity of 1.2 GW (1200 MW) at Abilene, under construction (completion mid-2026). Matches value and status.

facts.excerpt: 29 GE Vernova LM2500XPRESS gas turbines ... each capable of producing 34MW. If all turbines are installed, the total output would be 986MW.
facts.notes: Abilene campus: on-site natural gas microgrid (up to 986 MW from 29 GE Vernova turbines) plus ERCOT grid power including local wind. Crusoe adds 4.5 GW natural gas via Engine No. 1 JV (per Data Center Frontier). Domain tomshardware.com (press, not in explicit scoreable list).

verification.verifier_note: tomshardware article confirms natural-gas turbines (29 GE Vernova LM2500XPRESS, ~986 MW) deployed at Abilene as ADDITIONAL power because the ERCOT grid can't fully supply the site — i.e. natural gas + grid. Site under construction. Caveat: the article itself does not literally name 'wind'; wind is a

facts.excerpt: Stargate UAE is a 1-gigawatt compute cluster ... The first 200-megawatt AI cluster is expected to go live in 2026
facts.notes: Stargate UAE (Abu Dhabi), part of the 5GW UAE-U.S. AI Campus. Built by G42, operated by OpenAI and Oracle; Nvidia GB300 systems, plus Cisco, SoftBank. First cluster 200 MW; full cluster planned 1 GW (1000 MW). Recorded as cloud_partnership since OpenAI does not own the facility (G42-built/owned campus).

facts.excerpt: OpenAI signed an agreement with Oracle to buy $300 billion in computing power over a five-year period, beginning in 2027, which will supply 4.5 GW of capacity for OpenAI's Stargate buildout.
facts.notes: OpenAI-Oracle 4.5 GW additional Stargate capacity; ~$300 billion over five years starting 2027. Rented compute from Oracle = cloud_partnership. 4.5 GW announcement 2025-07-22 (OpenAI); $300B term reported Sep 2025. DCD direct fetch 403; quote via WebSearch of DCD/OpenAI.

verification.verifier_note: datacenterdynamics $300bn Oracle article (blocked WebFetch; read via DCD page content) states 'In July, OpenAI announced it was sourcing 4.5GW of data center capacity from Oracle.' Supports 4500 MW (4.5 GW) Oracle capacity.

facts.excerpt: Stargate Norway is planned to deliver 230MW of capacity, with ambitions to expand by an additional 290MW
facts.notes: Stargate Norway in Narvik (Kvandal), OpenAI's first European data center. 50/50 JV between Nscale and Aker (~$1B initial capital); OpenAI is offtaker/operator, does not own — recorded as cloud_partnership. Targeting 100,000 NVIDIA GPUs by end-2026 (company target, not a third-party tracker so not recorded as gpu_count). Base 230 MW + potential 290 MW expansion.

verification.verifier_note: from gap-review pass; pending blind verification

facts.excerpt: Powered entirely by renewable energy
facts.notes: Northern Norway hydropower; closed-loop direct-to-chip liquid cooling with heat reuse.

facts.notes: Phase 1 energized Sep 2025 for Oracle Cloud Infrastructure (OCI) serving OpenAI. Operated by Crusoe, not OpenAI-owned. Domain lancium.com (not in scoreable-domain list); Epoch AI corroborates 'four of the eight buildings already operational' at ~0.3 GW current.

facts.excerpt: OpenAI signed an agreement with Oracle to buy $300 billion in computing power over a five-year period, beginning in 2027
facts.notes: Dollar value of OpenAI's Oracle cloud commitment (~$300B, 5 years from 2027). Same deal as the 4.5 GW fact. Recorded separately as the USD figure. DCD fetch 403; quote via WebSearch.

verification.verifier_note: Same DCD article: OpenAI 'agreed to buy compute power worth $300 billion from Oracle in a five-year deal.' Supports $300B, contracted (reported). Note article frames it as a report.

facts.excerpt: at least 10 gigawatts of NVIDIA systems ... NVIDIA intends to invest up to $100 billion in OpenAI ... The first gigawatt of NVIDIA systems will be deployed in the second half of 2026
facts.notes: Letter of intent (announced). Nvidia to invest up to $100B in OpenAI progressively as each GW deploys; first GW 2H2026 on Vera Rubin platform. Jensen Huang told CNBC 10 GW ~= 4-5 million GPUs (not recorded as gpu_count: CEO statement, not a tracker).

verification.verifier_note: nvidianews.nvidia.com: 'deploy at least 10 gigawatts of NVIDIA systems' via letter of intent. Matches 10,000 MW announced.

facts.excerpt: brings Stargate to nearly 7 gigawatts of planned capacity and over $400 billion in investment over the next three years.
facts.notes: Five new sites announced with Oracle and SoftBank. openai.com direct fetch 403; quote captured via WebSearch of the OpenAI page. 'over $400 billion' recorded at conservative floor of 400000000000.

verification.verifier_note: five-new-stargate-sites page states 'over $400 billion in investment over the next three years.' Matches $400B announced/planned.

facts.excerpt: brings Stargate to nearly 7 gigawatts of planned capacity
facts.notes: Aggregate planned Stargate capacity across Abilene + five new sites + CoreWeave. Partner-operated (Crusoe/Vantage/STACK/SB Energy/Oracle), OpenAI is customer via Stargate JV, so recorded as cloud_partnership not owned capacity. 'nearly 7 GW' recorded at conservative 7000 MW.

verification.verifier_note: Same page: 'brings Stargate to nearly 7 gigawatts of planned capacity.' 7 GW = 7000 MW, planned/announced. Verified.

facts.excerpt: The agreement announced today brings the total contract value with OpenAI up to approximately $22.4 billion.
facts.notes: Updated CoreWeave total contract value. Existing dataset has the initial $11.9B (March 2025). Sept 25 2025 expansion added up to $6.5B (on top of a $4B May expansion), bringing cumulative total to ~$22.4B. OpenAI rents this capacity — cloud_partnership, not owned.

facts.excerpt: 6 gigawatt agreement to power OpenAI's next-generation AI infrastructure across multiple generations of AMD Instinct GPUs ... first 1 gigawatt deployment of AMD Instinct MI450 GPUs ... second half of
facts.notes: Definitive agreement. AMD issued OpenAI a warrant for up to 160M AMD shares vesting to 6 GW; potential ~$90B cumulative hardware revenue for AMD. amd.com direct fetch timed out; quote via WebSearch of AMD/OpenAI/SEC (SEC filing d28189dex991.htm).

verification.verifier_note: amd.com press release (WebFetch timed out; confirmed via AMD page title/content): 'AMD and OpenAI Announce Strategic Partnership to Deploy 6 Gigawatts of AMD GPUs,' definitive agreement scaling to 6 GW. Matches 6000 MW contracted.

facts.excerpt: 10 gigawatts of custom AI accelerator hardware ... OpenAI designs the chips; Broadcom manufactures them ... Deployment ... Starting in 2026 through 2029
facts.notes: OpenAI-Broadcom strategic collaboration announced 2025-10-13 to deploy 10 GW of OpenAI-designed AI accelerators; Broadcom develops/deploys with Ethernet networking; racks start 2H2026, complete by end 2029. FT estimated potential cost $350B-$500B (not disclosed). Custom silicon program = true.

verification.verifier_note: techcrunch (Oct 2025) confirms OpenAI-Broadcom partnership to design its own custom AI accelerators (10 GW of custom accelerator hardware), announced. Matches custom_silicon=true announced.

facts.excerpt: close to 1 gigawatt of AI capacity
facts.notes: 'Lighthouse' campus, Port Washington WI; Vantage $15B+ investment; 100% matched zero-emission energy (solar, wind, battery), 70% to campus; completion 2028. Primary source says 'close to 1 gigawatt' (recorded 1000 MW, conservative); Epoch projects 1.3 GW. Vantage-operated, OpenAI = customer.

Fact's status=under_cons

facts.excerpt: OpenAI contracted to purchase an incremental $250 billion of Azure services, with Microsoft no longer having a right of first refusal to be OpenAI's compute provider.
facts.notes: Signed 2025-10-28 alongside OpenAI for-profit recapitalization; Microsoft holds ~27% of the PBC. Microsoft no longer exclusive cloud provider. Rented Azure compute = cloud_partnership. Corroborated by GeekWire and MSFT 10-Q.

facts.notes: Reported late 2025: OpenAI runs part of ChatGPT inference on Google Cloud TPUs (Ironwood generation). No specific MW/dollar figure disclosed. Softer/reported deal; recorded as boolean cloud_partnership. Cross-referenced CNBC.

Status=operational is not supported by the cited page (it is a report of a

facts.excerpt: $38 billion agreement, which will have continued growth over the next seven years ... hundreds of thousands of state-of-the-art NVIDIA GPUs ... GB200s and GB300s
facts.notes: Seven-year deal; hundreds of thousands of Nvidia GB200/GB300 GPUs via EC2 UltraServers; all capacity targeted before end of 2026. Rented compute = cloud_partnership. Followed removal of Microsoft's cloud exclusivity.

verification.verifier_note: aboutamazon.com: 'Representing a $38B commitment, OpenAI will rapidly expand compute capacity.' Matches $38B contracted.

facts.notes: Third-party estimate by Epoch AI (H100-equivalents), for the Abilene Stargate site serving OpenAI. Projected 1.2 GW = ~1.0 million H100-equivalents at full build. Fleet estimator: Epoch AI.

facts.excerpt: OpenAI unveils its first custom chip, built by Broadcom
facts.notes: 'Jalapeño' - OpenAI's first custom AI (inference) chip, designed with Broadcom and manufactured by TSMC (TSMC confirmed via Digitimes/CNBC). First accelerator in a multi-generation platform; deployments begin late 2026. Value true = program exists.

verification.verifier_note: techcrunch (Jun 2026): OpenAI 'unveiled its first custom-built inference processor... Named Jalapeno,' built with Broadcom. Matches custom_silicon=true, Jalapeno chip, announced.
